name: Overlay
summary: An Overlay provides contextual model information directly in the viewport. This can be presented in 2D and 3D. Examples can be annotations or analytical model information such as geometry analysis, energy analysis, etc. The overlay feature is not intended as a mechanism to provide custom entities to SketchUp. Whatever overlays draw is not pickable nor exportable. It is also not allowed to perform model changes from overlay events. Doing so will result in a RuntimeError being thrown.

params.name: id
params.doc: The string should be unique per overlay subclass. A good pattern would be something like: “company_name.extension_name.overlay_name”.

params.name: name
params.doc: This is a user facing display name that will appear in the UI. Make it short and representative for what the overlay does.

params.name: description
params.doc: This is a user facing description that will appear in the UI. Make it short and representative for what the overlay does.

throws.when: if id or name is an empty string

methods.name: draw
methods.remarks: This method is abstract. It is called whenever the view updates. Note: This is called very often. Perform minimal amount of computation in this event. Cache the data needed to draw what the overlay needs whenever possible. Note: If you draw outside the model bounds you need to implement #getExtents which return a bounding box large enough to include the points you draw. Otherwise your drawing will be clipped.

params.name: view
params.doc: A View object where the method was invoked.

methods.name: getExtents
methods.remarks: This method is abstract. The method should be implementing sub-classes ensure what is drawn in 3D space doesn't appear clipped. If the overlay only draws in 2D this isn't needed. Note: This is called very often. Perform minimal amount of computation in this event. Cache the data needed to compute the bounds of what the overlay draws whenever possible. In order to accurately draw things, SketchUp needs to know the extents of what it is drawing. If the overlay is doing its own drawing, it may need to implement this method to tell SketchUp the extents of what it will be drawing. If you don't implement this method, you may find that part of what the overlay is drawing gets clipped to the extents of the rest of the model. This must return a Geom::BoundingBox. In a typical implementation, you will create a new Geom::BoundingBox, add points to set the extents of the drawing that the overlay will do and then return it.

methods.name: name
methods.summary: This is a user facing display name that will appear in the UI.
methods.remarks: This is a user facing display name that will appear in the UI.

methods.name: onMouseEnter
methods.remarks: This method is abstract. It can be used by implementing sub-classes to react to mouse movement in the viewport. The #onMouseEnter method is called by SketchUp when the mouse enters the viewport.

params.name: flags
params.doc: A bit mask that tells the state of the modifier keys and other mouse buttons at the time.

params.name: x
params.doc: The X coordinate on the screen where the event occurred.

params.name: y
params.doc: The Y coordinate on the screen where the event occurred.

methods.name: onMouseLeave
methods.remarks: This method is abstract. It can be used by implementing sub-classes to react to mouse movement in the viewport. The #onMouseLeave method is called by SketchUp when the mouse enters the viewport.

methods.name: onMouseMove
methods.signature: onMouseMove(flags, x, y, view) => Object | onMouseMove(flags, x, y, view) => Object
methods.remarks: This method is abstract. It can be used by implementing sub-classes to react to mouse movement in the viewport. Try to make this method as efficient as possible because this method is called often.

params.name: flags
params.doc: A bit mask that tells the state of the modifier keys and other mouse buttons at the time.

methods.name: source
methods.summary: Describes the source associated with the overlay.
methods.remarks: Describes the source associated with the overlay. This is automatically inferred when the overlay instance is initialized.

methods.name: start
methods.remarks: This method is abstract. It can be used by implementing sub-classes to react when the overlay becomes active, for instance when the user turns it on.

methods.name: stop
methods.remarks: This method is abstract. It can be used by implementing sub-classes to react when the overlay becomes inactive, for instance when the user turns it off.

methods.name: valid?
methods.summary: Indicates whether the overlay is valid.
methods.remarks: Indicates whether the overlay is valid. An overlay becomes invalid after being removed from the model and cannot be reused.

properties.name: description
properties.summary: This is a short user facing description of the overlay that will appear in the UI.
properties.remarks: This is a short user facing description of the overlay that will appear in the UI.

properties.name: enabled
properties.summary: Note: In most cases, extensions doesn't need to expose any new UI for enabling them.
properties.remarks: Note: In most cases, extensions doesn't need to expose any new UI for enabling them. This can be done from the Overlays panel. However, in some cases the extension might have additional UI related to the overlays and might want to offer a way to toggle its overlays along with the rest of the UI.
